sections.title: Foundational Synthesis — Metal Classification & Dual-Use Economics
required_elements: Metal classification on monetary-industrial spectrum (% monetary/store-of-value, industrial, jewelry, emerging/green energy)
Demand profile analysis and dual-use economics
Investment vehicle landscape mapping (physical, ETFs, futures, mining equities, streaming/royalty companies)

sections.title: Supply/Demand Fundamentals — The Physical Balance Sheet
required_elements: Supply architecture decomposition (mine production, recycling/secondary, government/institutional sales, by-product dynamics)
Supply elasticity assessment (new mine production response to price, development timeline)
Demand decomposition by end-market with volume and price sensitivity
Central bank/sovereign demand assessment
Supply/demand deficit or surplus analysis (trailing 3 years, forward 2-year estimate)
Identifiable above-ground inventory tracking (COMEX, LBMA, SHFE, ETF holdings)
Inventory-to-consumption ratio assessment (<3 months = tightness signal)

sections.title: Macro Regime & Price Driver Analysis
required_elements: Real interest rate sensitivity and inverse correlation analysis
Fed funds rate trajectory impact on precious metals
Dollar strength (DXY) correlation with metal prices
Inflation expectations (breakeven inflation, CPI, M2 growth) impact
Central bank gold reserve accumulation trend analysis
Current macro regime identification (risk-on, risk-off, stagflation, reflation)

sections.title: Financial Logic & Valuation
required_elements: Marginal cost of production analysis (90th percentile AISC as price floor)
Relative value vs. other metals (gold/silver ratio, gold/platinum, platinum/palladium mean-reversion analysis)
Real price (inflation-adjusted) vs. 20-year and 50-year averages
Inventory-adjusted supply/demand model with 2-year forward projection
Positioning-based analysis (CFTC COT data, net speculative positioning)

sections.title: Pattern Matching & IDP Flagging
required_elements: ETF outflows concurrent with rising spot price (physical market tightness indicator)
COMEX inventory drawdowns with rising open interest (delivery squeeze potential)
Mine production declining while demand grows (structural deficit formation)
Metal ratio extremes (gold/silver >80 or <50, mean-reversion opportunity)
Real rates falling while metal price flat (delayed response, catch-up rally potential)
Central bank buying acceleration (monetary regime shift signal)
Industrial demand exceeding mine supply growth (structural tightening)
Futures curve backwardation signal (physical market tightness)

sections.title: Investigation Tracks
required_elements: Physical market channel checks (dealer premiums, LBMA/COMEX inventory movements, mint production data)
Industrial demand verification (solar PV installation forecasts, automotive/electronics production data)
Macro regime positioning (CFTC COT data, ETF flows, central bank reserve disclosures, real rate modeling)

structural_requirements: All sections present with markdown headings
Metal classification on monetary-industrial spectrum clearly stated with percentages
Demand profile decomposed by application (jewelry, industrial fabrication, investment, central bank) with volumes
Investment vehicle types mapped (physical, ETFs, futures, mining equities, streaming)
Mine production tonnage and YoY growth documented with supply elasticity assessment
Recycling/secondary supply percentage calculated
By-product supply dynamics explained (% of total supply, price inelasticity)
Demand decomposition by end-market with price sensitivity by segment
Central bank buying/selling tracked with policy implications
Supply/demand deficit or surplus calculated (trailing 3 years and forward 2-year estimate)
COMEX registered and eligible inventory levels tracked with trend
ETF holdings as quasi-inventory documented
Inventory-to-consumption ratio calculated and assessed
Real interest rate sensitivity quantified (correlation analysis)
Dollar strength (DXY) impact documented
Inflation expectations (breakeven inflation) impact assessed
Macro regime identified (risk-on, risk-off, stagflation, reflation)
90th percentile AISC identified as price floor
Metal ratio analysis (gold/silver, gold/platinum) with mean-reversion thresholds
Real price vs. historical averages computed
CFTC COT positioning data analyzed (extremes identified)
Investigation tracks include specific data sources (LBMA inventory filings, mint production data, IEA forecasts)
